---
title: "Apples [vs.]{.vs} Oranges"
subtitle: "A comparison deck template"
author: "Your Name"
date: "2026-06-01"
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-eyebrow: "Comparison Deck"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: ../apples-oranges-revealjs.scss
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    margin: 0
    center: false
    slide-number: "c/t"
    code-line-numbers: false
    footer: "Apples & Oranges"
---

## Why compare? {.apple}

::: {.eyebrow}
Apples
:::

:::::: {.split}
::: {.points}
- [Two options, one decision]{.lead}<br>[Frame the choice up front so every slide ladders back to it.]{.desc}
- [Each side gets its own visual voice]{.lead}<br>[A dedicated palette per option keeps the two from blurring together.]{.desc}
- [Contrast keeps the audience oriented]{.lead}<br>[People always know which side they're looking at.]{.desc}
:::

:::: {.stack}
::: {.callout-note title="Note"}
Apple slides use pale text on a solid navy ground. Callouts sit on a desaturated navy-tint panel.
:::

```python
# choose a variety
variety = "honeycrisp"
```
::::
::::::

## Why compare? {.orange}

::: {.eyebrow}
Oranges
:::

:::::: {.split}
::: {.points}
- [Mirror structure, inverted palette]{.lead}<br>[Navy text on a pale ground sets the second option apart instantly.]{.desc}
- [Same layout, different emphasis]{.lead}<br>[The grid matches the apples side, so the audience compares like for like.]{.desc}
- [Easy to scan which side you're on]{.lead}<br>[The palette flip alone signals which option is on screen.]{.desc}
:::

:::: {.stack}
::: {.callout-note title="Tip"}
Orange slides invert the palette — navy text on a pale ground. Callouts use a desaturated pale panel.
:::

```python
# choose a variety
variety = "cara_cara"
```
::::
::::::

## Strengths & trade-offs {.compare}

::::: {.versus}
:::: {.side .apple}
[Apples]{.eyebrow}

[Strengths]{.sub}

- Crisp texture
- Long shelf life
- Great for baking

[Trade-offs]{.sub}

::: {.trade}
- Less juice per bite
- Fewer varieties in season
:::
::::

:::: {.side .orange}
[Oranges]{.eyebrow}

[Strengths]{.sub}

- High vitamin C
- Segment-friendly
- Bright, fresh flavor

[Trade-offs]{.sub}

::: {.trade}
- Peel can be messy
- Shorter peak freshness
:::
::::
:::::

## Neutral ground {.banana}

::: {.eyebrow}
Neutral
:::

:::::: {.split}
::: {.points .lg}
- [Use it for content that takes no side]{.lead}
- [Readable body on a soft, desaturated ground]{.lead}
- [Good for summaries, agenda, or context]{.lead}
:::

:::: {.stack}
::: {.swatches}
[apples]{.swatch .apple} [oranges]{.swatch .orange} [banana]{.swatch .banana}
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Note"}
Neutral slides sit outside the apples / oranges pairing — use them to set up or wrap up the comparison.
:::
::::
::::::

## Summary {.apple}

::: {.eyebrow}
Apples
:::

:::::: {.split}
::: {.points .lg}
- [Apples and Oranges pair across the two sides]{.lead}
- [Banana is the neutral, side-less tone]{.lead}
- [Same layout every time — only the palette flips]{.lead}
:::

:::: {.stack}
::: {.callout-note title="Use it for"}
Any two-option decision — products, plans, vendors, directions — where contrast helps the room choose.
:::

```python
# three tones
scheme = [apples, oranges, banana]
```
::::
::::::
